hero.title: THE COLLECTION
hero.subtitle: Twelve monolithic structures. Twelve global capitals. One architectural standard of silence and stone.

intro.title: A Century of Limestone
intro.description: From Auguste Perret's original vision on the Champs-Élysées to Zaha Hadid's desert masterpiece in Dubai, each LITHOS monolith carries the same philosophy: stone, silence, power.

cities.name: PARIS
cities.description: The original monolith where it all began. Completely restored in 2024 while preserving original limestone facades.
cities.longDescription: LITHOS Paris stands as the cornerstone of our collection. Commissioned in 1928 by the Société des Grands Hotels, Auguste Perret's masterpiece was the first to embody our philosophy of architectural silence. The building's iconic limestone facade, quarried from Château-Landon, has witnessed nearly a century of history while maintaining its timeless elegance. The 2024 restoration, led by Studio Perret, carefully preserved every original detail while introducing state-of-the-art acoustic isolation and discreet modern amenities.

cities.name: LONDON
cities.description: Raw concrete and limestone. Award-winning restoration of a 1960s landmark.
cities.longDescription: LITHOS London represents the perfect marriage of Brutalist architecture and LITHOS luxury. David Chipperfield's sensitive restoration of this 1965 landmark preserves the raw honesty of exposed concrete while introducing warm Portland stone interiors. The building's geometric facade creates ever-changing patterns of light and shadow throughout the day.

cities.name: TOKYO
cities.description: 47 floors of silence above the world's busiest intersection. Private helipad on level 45.
cities.longDescription: Tadao Ando's first skyscraper, LITHOS Tokyo rises above the neon chaos of Shinjuku like a silent sentinel. The building's signature exposed concrete, meticulously poured and finished, creates a sanctuary of calm in one of the world's most vibrant districts. Each suite features floor-to-ceiling windows with soundproofing that renders the city below completely silent.

cities.name: DUBAI
cities.description: The tallest of the collection. Fluid architecture inspired by desert dunes.
cities.longDescription: Zaha Hadid's final completed project, LITHOS Dubai is a masterpiece of parametric design. The building's flowing forms, inspired by the movement of desert sands, create a striking silhouette against Dubai's skyline. Inside, the seamless curves continue, with each suite offering panoramic views of the city and beyond.

cities.name: NEW YORK
cities.description: The newest addition. Rising above Manhattan with floor-to-ceiling limestone interiors.
cities.longDescription: LITHOS New York redefines luxury living in the sky. Rafael Viñoly's slender tower offers unparalleled views of Central Park and the Manhattan skyline. The building's exposed structural columns create a distinctive grid pattern, while the interiors feature floor-to-ceiling limestone and floor-to-ceiling windows that flood each space with natural light.

cities.name: SINGAPORE
cities.description: Sky gardens every 11 floors. Tropical modernism meets LITHOS limestone.
cities.longDescription: Moshe Safdie's LITHOS Singapore brings the outdoors in with its revolutionary biophilic design. Sky gardens every 11 floors create vertical oases of tropical vegetation, while the limestone-clad interiors maintain the signature LITHOS aesthetic. The building's open-air corridors and natural ventilation systems reduce energy consumption while connecting guests with Singapore's lush environment.

cities.name: ZURICH
cities.description: Swiss precision meets LITHOS silence. Thermal baths on the lower levels.
cities.longDescription: Peter Zumthor's only hotel project, LITHOS Zurich is a meditation on materiality and light. The building's minimalist facade conceals a world of thermal baths, steam rooms, and quiet contemplation. Each suite is a study in Swiss precision, with custom-designed furniture and fixtures that reflect the region's craft tradition.

cities.name: GENEVA
cities.description: Diplomatic sanctuary. Private meeting rooms for international delegations.
cities.longDescription: Mario Botta's geometric masterpiece in Geneva serves as a diplomatic sanctuary for international delegations. The building's distinctive striped facade of limestone and marble creates a powerful presence on Rue du Rhône, while the interior features multiple secure meeting rooms and translation facilities. Each suite offers views of either the Rhône or the Alps.

cities.name: MUMBAI
cities.description: Where the Arabian Sea meets LITHOS limestone. Opened 2024.
cities.longDescription: The latest addition to the collection, LITHOS Mumbai brings our philosophy to the Indian subcontinent. Pritzker laureate B.V. Doshi's design responds to the tropical climate with deep balconies, cross-ventilation, and shaded terraces. The building's limestone facade takes on a warm glow in the afternoon light, echoing the region's ancient stepwells and stone architecture.

cities.name: KYOTO
cities.description: Coming 2025. A modern interpretation of the traditional Japanese inn.
cities.longDescription: Scheduled to open in 2025, LITHOS Kyoto represents a dialogue between ancient and modern Japan. Kengo Kuma's design draws from the shoin-zukuri architectural tradition, with deep eaves, wooden lattices, and intimate garden views. The property will feature only 22 suites, each with its own private garden and onsen-style bath.

cities.name: SHANGHAI
cities.description: Coming 2026. A vertical garden overlooking the Huangpu River.
cities.longDescription: Set to open in 2026, LITHOS Shanghai will redefine luxury on the Bund. Wang Shu's design incorporates recycled materials and vertical gardens, creating a living building that changes with the seasons. The upper floors offer uninterrupted views of the Pudong skyline, while the lower levels connect to a new riverfront promenade.

cities.name: SYDNEY
cities.description: Coming 2027. Harbour views from every suite.
cities.longDescription: Scheduled for 2027, LITHOS Sydney will be the first Australian property in the collection. The late Kerry Hill's design celebrates the relationship between land and water, with every suite offering views of Sydney Harbour. The building's limestone facade will be quarried from South Australia, continuing our commitment to local materials.

architects.name: Auguste Perret
architects.bio: Pioneer of reinforced concrete construction and a master of French modernist architecture.

architects.name: David Chipperfield
architects.bio: Pritzker Prize-winning architect known for his sensitive restorations and minimalist aesthetic.

architects.name: Tadao Ando
architects.bio: Self-taught architect famous for his mastery of concrete and light.

architects.name: Zaha Hadid
architects.bio: First woman to win the Pritzker Prize, known for her fluid, futuristic forms.

architects.name: Rafael Viñoly
architects.bio: Known for his slender skyscrapers and innovative structural solutions.

architects.name: Moshe Safdie
architects.bio: Pioneer of modular construction and biophilic design.

architects.name: Peter Zumthor
architects.bio: Pritzker Prize winner known for his sensory architecture and material mastery.

architects.name: Mario Botta
notableWorks: San Francisco Museum of Modern Art
architects.bio: Known for his geometric forms and striped masonry facades.

architects.name: B.V. Doshi
architects.bio: First Indian architect to win the Pritzker Prize, pioneer of tropical modernism.

architects.name: Kengo Kuma
architects.bio: Known for his integration of traditional Japanese elements with modern design.

architects.name: Wang Shu
notableWorks: Campus of China Academy of Art
architects.bio: Pritzker Prize winner known for his use of recycled materials and traditional techniques.

architects.name: Kerry Hill
architects.bio: Master of resort architecture and tropical modernism.

brands.name: LITHOS GRAND
brands.description: Flagship ultra-luxury hotels in global capitals
brands.longDescription: The original LITHOS brand, established in 1928 with the opening of LITHOS Paris. Each LITHOS GRAND property is a architectural landmark, designed by world-renowned architects and carved from local limestone. These are our signature properties, offering the complete LITHOS experience: absolute silence, uncompromising privacy, and service that anticipates every need.

brands.name: THE EMBER COLLECTION
brands.longDescription: A collection of intimate properties in emerging destinations. Each EMBER hotel tells a unique story, celebrating local culture and craftsmanship while maintaining LITHOS standards of service and comfort. Perfect for the discerning traveler seeking authentic experiences.

brands.name: SILENCE Retreats
brands.longDescription: Dedicated to holistic wellness and digital detox. SILENCE Retreats are located in the world's most peaceful locations - from the Himalayas to the Patagonian Andes. Each retreat offers personalized wellness programs, ancient healing rituals, and complete disconnection from the outside world.

brands.name: LIMESTONE Heritage
brands.description: Historic palace conversions and heritage properties
brands.longDescription: Preserving architectural heritage while creating luxurious accommodations. LIMESTONE Heritage properties include converted palaces, castles, and historic mansions, each meticulously restored to its former glory. These properties offer a unique glimpse into the past while providing modern luxury.

brands.name: LITHOS Residences
brands.longDescription: For those who wish to make LITHOS their home. LITHOS Residences offer the same architectural excellence and service standards as our hotels, but with the privacy and permanence of a private residence. Available for purchase or long-term lease in select locations.

brands.name: THE INNER CIRCLE
brands.description: Private membership club with global privileges
brands.longDescription: By invitation only. THE INNER CIRCLE offers the ultimate expression of LITHOS luxury. Members enjoy priority access, exclusive experiences, and privileges across all LITHOS properties and brands. Includes personal concierge, private events, and access to members-only spaces.
